message.content:
@startuml
skinparam backgroundColor #FFFFFF
skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center

participant "LCM" as LCM <<actor>>
box "#LightGreen Class\nApplicationProperty" 
  participant "" as AP1 hidden
end box
box "#Lavender Class\nDscResourceBase" 
  participant "" as DRB1 hidden
end box
box "#Lavender Function\nConvertTo-HashtableFromObject" 
  participant "" as CTHFO1 hidden
end box
box "#Lavender Function\nTest-DscParameterState" 
  participant "" as TDPS1 hidden
end box

activate LCM
AP1 ->> AP2 : ApplicationProperty.Test()
note right of AP2: (([DscResourceBase] $this).Test())
DRB1 ->> DRB3 : "$this"
CTHFO1 -->> DRB4 : Return Type: [Hashtable]
DRB5 <-< DRB6 : '$actual = $this.Get()'
CTHFO2 --> DRB7 : '$actual'
TDPS1 <-- DRB8 : 'Return Type: [Boolean]'
note over TDPS1,DSCRB9: DscResourceBase must determine which properties are optional to exclude them from comparison.\nNote that this function can be overridden in derived classes if needed for more complex scenarios like nested objects or arrays etc.. Note also there may exist other ways than using Test method directly such as calling GetTargetResource first then comparing results manually but those approaches would require additional coding effort compared against built-in functionality provided by base class implementation here demonstrated above where we see how easy things become once proper abstractions have been put into place through inheritance hierarchies designed specifically around common patterns found across many different types resources managed within PowerShell Desired State Configuration framework itself! 

@enduml
